title: App context — Actian product surface area
description: Schema for the app-context domain — validates the CI-derived consolidated output `app-context/dist/app-context.json`.

_schema_version.description: Substrate schema version. Pinned at 1; bump only on a schema-incompatible change to the file shape.

_meta.description: Provenance stamp on the CI-derived consolidated file. Present only on the derived dist output; absent on hand-authored src files.

examples.do_not_edit: Edit the app-context/src/ files; CI regenerates this file.

auto_generated.description: True when this file was produced by the derive script rather than hand-authored.

source.description: Relative path (from repo root) of the script that generated this file.

do_not_edit.description: Guidance string signalling that manual edits will be overwritten on next derive run; edit the src files instead.
examples: Edit the app-context/src/ files; CI regenerates this file.

_regen.description: Optional provenance block describing how this file is regenerated. See $defs.regenBlock.

apps.description: Catalogue of Actian apps keyed by app slug. Each entry describes the app's purpose, primary users, header chrome, sidebar navigation, and routing signals.

entities.description: Catalogue of first-class domain entities keyed by entity slug. Each entry describes the entity's label, definition, attribute set, and which apps surface it.

terminology.description: Approved-vs-discouraged vocabulary keyed by term slug. Each entry pins the canonical spelling, meaning, and aliases to avoid. Used by content validators and copy review.

patterns.description: Catalogue of recurring UX patterns keyed by pattern slug. Each entry describes the pattern's intent and which apps it appears in. Used by flow generation to pick app-appropriate compositions.

regenBlock.description: Structured provenance block describing how this file is regenerated (or that it is hand-curated). Shared across Class C JSONs in this substrate.

source.description: Where the canonical content of this file comes from. `manual` = hand-curated; `figma` = synced from a Figma node; `ai-assisted` = LLM-generated with human review.

lastSync.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent sync from `source`. Set by sync tooling for `figma`/`ai-assisted` sources; omit for `manual`.

instructions.description: Free-form prose telling a human author or AI assistant how to update this file responsibly. Surfaced by the Knowledge Editor's 'Regenerate with AI' seam.
examples: Manually curated; update by hand as the product evolves.

nodeId.description: Figma node id (with a colon separator), when source = `figma`.

app.description: Description of a single Actian app surface.

label.description: Display name of the app as shown in product chrome and docs.

purpose.description: One-paragraph statement of what the app is for and what jobs it covers.
examples: Data governance, catalog management, stewardship, lineage.

users.description: Primary user roles served by this app.

header.description: Header chrome configuration for this app.

type.description: Header variant identifier consumed by flow generators (matches the DS `global-header` `App type` axis).

sidebar.description: Ordered list of sidebar nav entries shown in this app. Empty array when the app has no left-nav (e.g. Explorer).

id.description: Stable slug used for routing and analytics.

signals.description: Keywords/phrases that, when present in a user request, route work to this app. Used by skill routing to pick an app context.

useCases.description: What the app is FOR, framed as audience → jobs, optionally linked to app-scoped patterns. Drives flow framing + scaffold selection in consumers.

audience.description: Primary users this use case serves.

jobs.description: Jobs-to-be-done for this audience in this app.

patterns.description: App-scoped pattern slugs idiomatic to this use case; existence + app-scope enforced by validate-app-context.js.

entity.description: Description of a single domain entity.

examples: Curated, business-ready asset with Input/Output Ports.

properties.description: Common attributes/fields the entity carries. Each item is a bare field name (legacy) or a typed descriptor for realistic generated content.

name.description: Field name as shown in the UI.

type.description: Logical type of the field, used to shape generated form controls and table columns.

example.description: Realistic example value(s) for generated content.

states.description: Enumerated states, when the type is enum.

relationships.description: Map of relationship name → target entity slug. Keys are verbs like `hasMetadata`, `belongsTo`; values are entity slugs from the `entities` map.

apps.description: App slugs (from the `apps` map) where this entity is surfaced.

use.description: The approved spelling/phrasing to use in product copy and docs.

meaning.description: What the term denotes — enough to disambiguate.

notUse.description: Aliases/synonyms to avoid. Content guardrails flag these in generated copy.

pattern.description: Description of a recurring UX pattern.

description.description: One-paragraph statement of when and how to use the pattern.
examples: Opens from list row click. Studio: quick edit. Explorer: quick view.

apps.description: App slugs (from the `apps` map) where the pattern appears.
